problem_addressed: Lack of comprehensive quantitative evaluation for LLMs acting as agents in interactive environments.
core_claim: While top commercial LLMs show strong agent capabilities, significant performance gaps exist with open-source models, primarily due to poor long-term reasoning, decision-making, and instruction following abilities.
key_contributions: AGENTBENCH: A multi-dimensional benchmark with 8 distinct environments for evaluating LLM-as-Agent capabilities
Extensive evaluation of 29 API-based and open-source LLMs revealing performance disparities
Identification of key failure modes and obstacles in LLM agent development
novelty: First comprehensive benchmark specifically designed for evaluating LLMs as agents across diverse interactive environments, unlike prior work focusing on single-task evaluations.
method_summary: AGENTBENCH creates 8 simulated interactive environments (Operating System, Database, Knowledge, Graph, Digital Card Game, Lateral Thinking Puzzle, House-holding, Web) where LLMs perform agent tasks, with performance measured through automated evaluation.
method_components: Eight distinct interactive environments covering different agent challenges
Automated evaluation framework measuring task completion success
Comparative analysis across API-based and open-source LLMs
key_innovation: Multi-environment design capturing diverse agent capabilities (reasoning, decision-making, instruction following) beyond traditional benchmarks, revealing critical failure modes in LLM agents.
assumptions: Simulated environments adequately represent real-world agent challenges
LLM outputs can be objectively evaluated through automated metrics
Performance gaps reflect fundamental limitations rather than evaluation artifacts
theoretical_grounding: None presented; relies on empirical analysis of performance data and failure patterns across environments.
datasets_used: FREEBASE-based dataset with 1,663 questions from GrailQA, ComplexWebQuestions, and GraphQuestions
Text-to-SQL dataset with INSERT, UPDATE, and SELECT operations
title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
